rename-and-remove-tracked-files.label: Rename and Remove Tracked Files
rename-and-remove-tracked-files.summary: Change tracked paths with Git-aware operations, review the staged shape, and record one structural cleanup.

sketch3.title: Review and Record the New Shape

try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.title: Rename a Tracked Guide
try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `release-kit/` repository.

Rename `docs/guide-draft.md` to `docs/volunteer-guide.md` with Git, then inspect short status.
try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.hint: Move the tracked path through Git so the structural change is staged.

help.concept: git mv changes the working tree path and stages the removal/addition pair as one intentional rename.
help.hint_1: Confirm that the new path exists and the old path no longer appears as tracked.
help.hint_2: Review the staged name-status summary before committing.

try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.solutionCode:
git mv docs/guide-draft.md docs/volunteer-guide.md
git status --short

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf release-kit
mkdir -p release-kit
git -C release-kit init -q -b main
git -C release-kit config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git -C release-kit config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
cd release-kit
mkdir -p docs
printf %s '# Draft Guide
' > docs/guide-draft.md
cd ..
cd release-kit
mkdir -p docs
printf %s 'Old checklist
' > docs/old-checklist.md
cd ..
git -C release-kit add -A
git -C release-kit commit -q -m 'Add project baseline'

try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.title: Remove an Obsolete Checklist
try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `release-kit/` repository.

Remove `docs/old-checklist.md` with Git and verify that the deletion is staged.
try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.hint: Remove the obsolete tracked path through Git so the deletion is staged.

help.concept: git rm removes a tracked file from the working tree and stages that deletion for the next commit.
help.hint_1: Earlier commits will still preserve the old file.
help.hint_2: Verify the deletion alongside any related rename before recording the cleanup.

try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.title: Review and Commit the Cleanup
try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.prompt: You are working in the prepared `release-kit/` repository. The guide rename and obsolete-file removal are staged. Review the staged name-status summary, commit the cleanup as `Organize release documentation`, and confirm a clean state.
try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.hint: Confirm the structural boundary before recording it.

help.concept: A staged name-status summary proves the repository shape before the cleanup enters history.
help.hint_1: The rename and removal serve one documentation cleanup.
help.hint_2: After committing, short status should be quiet.

try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.solutionCode:
git diff --staged --name-status
git commit -m "Organize release documentation"
git status --short

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf release-kit
mkdir -p release-kit
git -C release-kit init -q -b main
git -C release-kit config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git -C release-kit config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
cd release-kit
mkdir -p docs
printf %s '# Draft Guide
' > docs/guide-draft.md
cd ..
cd release-kit
mkdir -p docs
printf %s 'Old checklist
' > docs/old-checklist.md
cd ..
git -C release-kit add -A
git -C release-kit commit -q -m 'Add project baseline'
git -C release-kit mv docs/guide-draft.md docs/volunteer-guide.md
git -C release-kit rm -q docs/old-checklist.md

git -C release-kit mv docs/guide-draft.md docs/volunteer-guide.md

single-choice-1.title: Purpose of git mv
single-choice-1.prompt: What is the primary purpose of the `git mv` command?
single-choice-1.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Use Git-aware structural operations, review the staged paths, and record a cleanup without losing earlier versions.
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: To move a file without tracking
options.b: To rename a file and stage the change

single-choice-2.title: Effect of git rm
single-choice-2.prompt: What happens when you use the `git rm` command?
single-choice-2.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Use Git-aware structural operations, review the staged paths, and record a cleanup without losing earlier versions.
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: The file is moved to a new location
options.b: The file is removed from the working directory and staged for deletion

multi-choice-1.title: Commands to Verify Staged Changes
multi-choice-1.prompt: Which commands can you use to verify staged changes before committing?
multi-choice-1.hint: Judge each option independently; choose only the statements that stay true in this exact repository state.

help.concept: Use Git-aware structural operations, review the staged paths, and record a cleanup without losing earlier versions.
help.hint_1: A partly true option is still wrong if it adds an effect Git does not guarantee.
help.hint_2: Use the layer named in the prompt—working tree, staging area, or history—to test each choice.

drag-reorder-1.title: Steps to Rename and Commit a File
drag-reorder-1.prompt: Arrange the steps to rename a file and commit the change using Git.
drag-reorder-1.hint: Start with the state that must exist before the next action can make sense.

help.concept: Use Git-aware structural operations, review the staged paths, and record a cleanup without losing earlier versions.
help.hint_1: Track how the project moves from working files to a proposed snapshot and then to history.
help.hint_2: Read the ordered steps as one continuous repository story.

tokens.t1: Use git mv to rename the file
tokens.t2: Check the status with git status
tokens.t3: Commit the change with git commit

sketch0.title: Rename a Tracked Path
sketch0.bodyMarkdown:
Rename a tracked path through Git:

```bash
git mv docs/guide-draft.md docs/volunteer-guide.md
```

The working tree changes and the rename is staged together. Earlier commits still preserve the old path, so the project keeps its story.

sketch1.title: Remove a Tracked Path
sketch1.bodyMarkdown:
When a file no longer belongs in the project, remove it through Git:

```bash
git rm docs/old-checklist.md
```

The deletion is staged for the next snapshot. The file remains available in earlier commits; current history is not erased.

sketch3.title: Review and Record the New Shape
sketch3.bodyMarkdown:
Structural changes are easiest to review as names and statuses:

```bash
git diff --staged --name-status
```

Confirm the rename and deletion, then commit them together only when they serve the same cleanup purpose. Finish with short status rather than introducing another file-list command.
